phases.summary: The Netherlands settled immediately into a possession-dominant rhythm, spending most of the opening minutes on the ball and taking control after Tunisia’s early left-flank attack. Tunisia appeared to sit deeper and try to break out or deliver early wide balls, but the Dutch were efficient enough to punish the opening phase with two quick goals and little sustained threat against them.

phases.summary: The Netherlands increasingly took control of the half, pinning Tunisia back with long spells of final-third possession, repeated corners, and pressure around the box. Tunisia’s best route was the quick counter or wide delivery, but the Dutch back line and Verbruggen handled those moments well, while the visitors struggled to convert their few promising breaks into clean chances.

The text only gives the scoreline and the match’s major incidents, not enough to identify specific shapes or tactical patterns. Tunisia were undone early by an own goal from Ellyes Skhiri, and the Netherlands quickly added a second through Brian Brobbey, putting the game in a one-sided state by half-time.

phases.summary: The second half opened with the Netherlands still controlling territory and creating chances from wide areas, cutbacks and corners, while Tunisia were increasingly pinned back and forced into clearances and blocks. Tunisia briefly responded with Mastouri’s 54' goal, but the Netherlands restored control almost immediately through sustained pressure and then a second goal from a corner-related phase before both teams made substitutions to refresh the shape and tempo.

adjustments: Tunisia made a fourth substitution: Firas Chaouat for Ismael Gharbi
Netherlands made a fourth substitution: Memphis Depay for Brian Brobbey
Netherlands made their fifth and final substitution: Noa Lang for Cody Gakpo
Tunisia made their final substitution: Sebastian Tounekti for Hazem Mastouri

Tunisia’s Mejbri versus Verbruggen: Mejbri’s long-range effort was tipped over by the Dutch goalkeeper
phases.summary: This late phase was defined more by substitutions and game management than by open tactical reshaping, with both teams using their remaining changes as the clock wound down. Tunisia threatened through Mejbri’s distance shot, but the Netherlands stayed in control, survived the late set-piece moments, and saw out a 3-1 win to finish top of Group F.
